title: Loops
summary: Repeat actions efficiently using for and while loops

sections.title: Loops as a Washing Machine Cycle
sections.content: A washing machine runs the same cycle over and over until the timer reaches zero. Each spin, rinse, and drain is one iteration. Java loops work the same way: they repeat a block of code until a condition tells them to stop. The for loop is like a washing machine with a preset program — you know exactly how many cycles you need. The while loop is like waiting for bread to toast — you keep checking until it's done, but you don't know exactly how long it will take.

sections.title: Counting with a For Loop
sections.content: This for loop prints the multiplication table for the number 5

sections.title: While Loops and When to Use Each
sections.content: Use a for loop when you know the exact number of repetitions, like printing all students in a class of 30. Use a while loop when the repetitions depend on a condition that might change, like waiting for a user to type 'quit'. A common pattern is the 'sentinel loop': keep asking for input until the user enters a special value. The do-while variant always runs the body at least once, like checking a restaurant menu — you always look at it, but whether you order depends on what you see.

sections.title: Creating an infinite loop by accident
sections.content: Most common mistake: forgetting to update the loop variable, like writing int i = 1; while (i <= 10) { System.out.println(i); } without i++. The condition never becomes false, so the loop runs forever. Fix: always ensure your loop variable changes toward the exit condition. Wrong: while (count < 5) { System.out.println("hi"); }. Correct: while (count < 5) { System.out.println("hi"); count++; }

sections.title: Your turn
sections.prompt: A gym tracks daily visitors. You have an array of visitor counts for 7 days: [45, 52, 38, 61, 47, 55, 42]. Write a loop that calculates and prints the total visitors for the week and the average per day.
hints: Which loop type works best when you know the array length?
How do you add each day's count to a running total?
How do you calculate an average from a total?
